comments.content:
[IMAGE:0]It's just about midnight as I type this, so here we go with a new Magic Monday. This week's classic book of Western occultism is Self-Unfoldment by Disciplines of Realization by the inimitable Manly Palmer Hall, the most influential American occultist of the 20th century. Hall wrote scores of books and hundreds of pamphlets, some much more famous than this one, but to my mind this is his single best work, a clear, thoughtful, and balanced guide to meditation and spiritual development. It's currently out of print, though I hope some enthusiasm for it can change that; combine it with the Knapp-Hall tarot deck, which Hall helped design, and the notes on practice Hall slipped into the booklet that goes with the deck, and you have a tolerably complete guide to the method of meditation Hall taught to his inner students. 

Ask me anything about occultism and I'll do my best to answer it. Any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 143,916th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.0 of The Magic Monday FAQ here.

I've had several people ask about tipping me for answers here, and though I certainly don't require that I won't turn it down. You can use the button below to access my online tip jar. If you're interested in political and economic astrology, or simply prefer to use a subscription service to support your favorite authors, you can find my Patreon page here and my SubscribeStar page here.

With that said, have at it!

***This Magic Monday is now closed -- and yes, this means you. See you next Monday!***

replies.title: Canker sores
replies.content: The only evidence I have is anecdotal, and this is absolutely not to be construed as medical advice, and just in case, I am keeping this post anonymous.  Canker sores, warts, and other herpes-type inflammations, tend to be the result of not getting enough zinc.  Zinc is hard to get in the first place -- diet-wise, it only tends to proliferate in strawberries and pumpkin seeds -- so I regularly supplement by sucking on zinc lozenges such as Cold-Eeze and Sambucus.  The zinc in the lozenges coats your throat, kills the bad stuff and keeps the good, helps you not to be so prone to the flu and colds, etc.  Just make certain you don't suck on a zinc lozenge on an empty stomach, because it can cause nausea.

I've recently come back from a family trip to Hawaii, and have a few questions which emerge out of that.

1) I experienced what I'll call subtle plane jet lag: much as my physical body was disoriented, so too were my subtle bodies. Hawaii and Eastern Canada are quite different, so it makes sense my subtle body would be thrown off quite a bit, as well as my physical body, but it raises an intriguing possibility: perhaps, much as frequent travellers often seem to end up unable to establish steady circadian rhythms, frequent travel makes it hard to establish a link to the land one lives on. This would help explain the alienation so many people feel towards home. Does this make sense? Can you think of ways to investigate this?

2) I had a fairly powerful experience while in Hawaii. I suspect it was an experience of Pele, the Hawaiian goddess of the volcano. There was a sign which said something like we all need to help take care of Mauna Loa and Kilauea. The experience I had was of something outside my me sarcastically saying “Like I need you to take care of me!” and then seeing an image of an eruption covering the entire island.

This experience was somewhat new to me, and so I'm curious about a few things now:

a) Is there a way to check if this was Pele, or someone or something else?

b) Do you know if people outside Hawaii, without Hawaiian heritage have ever had strong experiences of the Hawaiian gods? In particular, do you know if they are limited to Hawaii, or if it's possible to reach out to them from here?

c) If I do this, will it increase my chances of ending up living in Hawaii, either this lifetime or another? Given what I know of the islands, I'm not especially optimistic about their future in the short term. Or is this a case where faith is important, trusting that the gods will lead me where I'm best suited?

4) Finally, I seem to have a strong need for the telluric current. Am I correct to think a volcano is a very potent source of it, and this might explain why I felt amazing while I was there, pretty much from the moment I got there?

comments.title: Inner Alchemy
comments.content:
Dear JMG, thanks again for hosting Magic Mondays.

First an announcement: I’ve made a list of all JMG’s interviews, podcasts and public speeches that I could find online. There are over 50 records from 2005 to 2020. The real question is how does JMG find time to sleep? Anyone interested can find the list here: https://ecosophian.dreamwidth.org/429.html

Now, to the actual questions:

1. About SOP. I’ve long wondered why the elements are located the way they are: water opposing air and fire opposing earth. It I know you say that’s just the way they are, but I wanted to find some logic in here; why doesn’t fire oppose water and air oppose earth?

So I recently stumbled onto this page http://kheper.net/topics/Jung/typology.html, where the 4 functions of the Ego are discussed. I see this diagram: 

           Intuition
              |
Feeling ------|------ Thinking
              |
           Sensation

I think to myself: “this reminds me of something”. I know that Air associated with Thinking and water with emotions aka with Feeling. I remembered that some books on Alchemy equated intuition with Fire. So, I flipped the horizontal axis and drew the chart like this:

             South
           Intuition
             Fire
East           |          West
Thinking -----Ego------ Feeling
Air            |          Water
             North                
           Sensation
             Earth

There, now it makes more sense. The four elements we work with are the four functions of the Ego. So, in the center is the Ego which must then equate to the Spirit Within. But what about the Spirit Below and the Spirit Above? I assume they are associated with the Id (chthonic Spirit Below) and the Super-Ego (solar Spirit Above) of the Sigmund Freud's structural model of the psyche.

Am I on the right track with this sort of thinking? If I am, still the question remains, what about Extraversion and Introversion? They are not worked with in the ritual, maybe they are not ego functions.

2. While looking for the records of your speeches, JMG, I found the one where you talk about alchemical initiations. That made me think about SOP again - that it has deep alchemical meaning, I mean we break down the self, strengthen (invoke) and purify (banish) the parts and then reassemble it into something better and stronger. After countless repetitions it might become divinity manifest i.e. an inner philosopher’s stone! So, the Sphere of Protection is a way of making the philosopher’s stone for inner alchemy. Is my line of thinking correct?

P.S. In that speech you also defined the ritual space as a Mesocosm, an intermediary between the Microcosm and the Macrocosm. It connected some dots for me i.e. how magic works not just on myself but on the world as well.

replies.title: Re: Inner Alchemy
replies.content:
0) Thank you for doing this!

1) This is a good solid meditation exercise of the sort that traditional occult schools used to assign their students. There are no right answers and no wrong ones -- the point is to work out an arrangement that makes sense to you, and in the process, learn more about how to assemble the world into coherent patterns.

2) Same thing here. You've drawn a connection; that connection isn't right and it isn't wrong -- it's a way of stretching your mind to improve your ability to synthesize ideas into a world of meaning. I'd say keep working with it!

3) Glad to hear it.

replies.title: Re: Inner Alchemy
replies.content:
Thank you for your reply, Archdruid.

"There are no right answers and no wrong ones" this line does bother me a lot for some reason. We're not operating in the world of pure make believe, at least I hope we're not, so there is some objective reality that exists and my ideas can approximate it, some better some worse, so they are not all of the same value.

I get it that you want me to think for myself, nobody is going to do it for me and it will do me good to practice meditation.

I wonder though how far can I 'stretch my mind'? If I do physical exercise there's only so much muscle I can build on my body, the law of diminishing returns applies. Is it the same for the ability to 'synthesize ideas into the world of meaning"?

replies.title: Re: Inner Alchemy
replies.content:
There is an objective reality, but human beings are incapable of experiencing it. As Goethe wrote, alles vergangliche ist nur ein gleichnis -- all that belongs to the world of change is merely a symbol. In meditation you're taking your first baby steps toward becoming consciously aware of the mental plane of being...and in the usual taxonomy, there are three entire planes of being beyond the mental plane of which we can conceive nothing.

When you're talking about the interrelationships of symbols, above all, deciding that one set of correspondences is right and others are wrong is profoundly unhelpful. Symbols are useful, not true; truth belongs to what they represent, and -- ahem -- the map is not the territory.

replies.title: Re: Inner Alchemy
replies.content: Very well then. Thank you for clarifying that.

replies.title: Re: Inner Alchemy
replies.content:
In Antiquity and the Middle Ages there were two principla ways of assigning the elements to the pointsof the compass. The one you illustrated, with Earth in the North and Water in the West, derives from the ancient way by which the elements are assigned to the signs of the zodiac.

The other one, with Water in the North and Earth in the West, derives from the back-and-forth between the ancient defining binary properties of the elements (hot/cold and dry/wet), which produce a circular pattern of Fire-Earth-Water-Air (-Fire-Earth-etc.). Fire is hot and dry, earth is dry and cold, water is cold and wet, and air is wet and hot.

I think of them as the celestial and the terrestrial element-assignments respectively. Mysdelf, I mostly work with the terrestrial assignment, as my natal chart is heavy with Earth signs. The terrestrial assignment can also can work as a ladder of ascent or descent from lower fire to upper fire, through the other three elements: Earth rests upon (lower) fire (e.g. magma), water rests upon earth, air upon water, and (upper) fire upon air (e.g. the Sun).

replies.content:

Jez

Hi JMG,

I have a question regarding the distance one should keep to the dead.

Part of my regular practice is this routine:
1) Christian banishing (cross in four directions...) of the working space
2) Praying the rosary
3) Then I pray another rosary for the benefit of my dead relatives while imagining them being with me in the room. Afterwards I speak to them, pretty much in the mental mode of prayer: telling them about my worries, expressing hope that they are doing fine and asking them to look out for me and my family.

As part of the third step I began recently to imagine giving my deceased grandmother a long embrace. Doing this is something I neglected doing while she was alive and I feel pretty bad about it. I felt a strong sense of her presence while doing that.

You have warned against invoking the dead in the past and I'm not sure if what I'm doing might be unsafe or even dangerous. As much as I like my departed I'm not to keen on joining them too soon...

Thank you!

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: No, what you're doing is fine -- people in most cultures do this as a matter of course. (It gets labeled "ancestor worship" by those who don't understand the difference between worship and other modes of interacting with the disembodied.) The things to avoid are, first, necromancy -- summoning the spirits of the dead into any more than a purely imaginal presence -- and second, spiritualism -- allowing the spirits of the dead to possess you. Both of those lead in very unpleasant directions. (And doing the Christian banishing ritual, btw, should keep you safe from any movement in that direction.)

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) You -- meaning here your conscious personality -- doesn't integrate the shadow into the Self; the shadow and the Self engage in a process between themselves, which the personality at most watches from outside. One of Jung's important points is that the personality really doesn't have that much influence over what happens in the unconscious, and in most cases its best bet is to stand back and watch. The SoP is useful in that it provides a form through which the archetype of the Self can easily manifest, but much of what follows is not your job.

2) Depends. Some people get very good results from it, others don't.

3) Good heavens, of course you can read other books. If you want to be a well-educated occultist, you should certainly do that.

replies.title: (no subject)

replies.content: The SoP is itself a mandala, and as Jung points out, all mandalas constellate the archetype of the Self. How that will play out in your own psyche depends on wholly personal factors.

11. Perfection by occupation, conjunction, mutation, translation with unfavourable figure - you could fail to act this but you will regret it (therefore you should do it).

The request revolves around your commentary on the lunar eclipse of 10th Jan 2020. As I write this on Sunday the 23rd Feb, the COVID-19 virus appears to have tipped over into a pandemic. replies.title: Re: On perfection, and a request.
replies.content:
1) An unfavorable aspect with an unfavorable figure is screaming at you, "No, no, don't do it!"

2) I don't recommend using questions such as "should I refrain from doing X" -- those are very tricky to analyze, precisely because of the complexities involving a negative of a negative. If you always reframe your question so that it's always "should I do X?" you'll get clearer answers and have less trouble with interpretation.

3) The Chinese government is extremely edgy about predictions using astrology or other metaphysical methods that have to do with Chinese politics and society. (To be fair, this has been true of Chinese governments for a couple of millennia.) I don't cast ingress charts for Beijing because I don't want my Chinese readers to be at risk, and I don't feel that it's a good idea to depart from that policy in this case. As you clearly have the knowledge necessary to delineate an ingress chart, I'd encourage you to do the same for the eclipse chart and draw your own conclusions.

comments.title: Differences in Pentagram Tracing
comments.content:
I started reading "Monsters," and discovered to my surprise that the banishing pentagram given there is different from the one in Celtic Golden Dawn, which I've been practicing for some time now. I was interested to learn that "each of the ten ways of tracing a pentagram has a different magical effect," so I gather that's what's going on, but am curious to know more. I'm assuming I should keep doing it as described in CGD ("from the topmost point down and to the left, and then around counterclockwise to the same point") but am curious about the difference. Especially interesting that the banishing pentagram instructions in "Monsters" are going in the same direction as the summoning pentagram in CGD, albeit starting at a different point. Thanks for any enlightening elaboration you can provide!

Also you mention in "Monsters" that washing in cold water helps keep "larvae" and other low-grade entities for parasitizing you. What exactly are etheric larvae?

replies.title: Re: Differences in Pentagram Tracing
replies.content:
1) The version of the pentagram given in Monsters is from the Hermetic GD, and reflects that tradition's roots in Christian spirituality -- you're always banishing the element of Earth, which in Christian teaching is considered morally suspect at best and actively evil most of the time. Druids don't think that way. The Druidical GD starts from the point of spirit, going clockwise to open the portals to bring spirit closer to matter and counterclockwise to reverse the process and let spirit and matter settle back into their ordinary relationship; if you're working with the CGD material, that's the way you should do it.

2) The word "larva" meant a kind of spirit long before scientists borrowed it as a name for an immature insect. An etheric larva is a very simple, rather primitive being that has an etheric body but not a physical body; in the ecosystem of the etheric plane, it fills a necessary niche, as it goes around feeding on cast-off etheric bodies and other lumps of concentrated etheric substance that aren't attached to living things any more. (Thus it does on its plane what jackals and vultures do on the material plane.) Etheric larvae are dumber than bricks, though, and if your etheric body is damaged -- something that happens fairly often in the ordinary conditions of modern life -- one can blunder into a ragged part of your etheric body and start chewing on it, draining some of your life force. Cold water absorbs excess etheric substance, and so larvae don't like being around it -- that's why the seashore and places with cold mountain rivers feel so enlivening. Washing daily with cold water is a good way to keep your etheric body free of them.

(I hope this is not a sensitive question for Freemasons :))

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
It's a common misconception that Freemasonry has a theology of its own. Individual Freemasons do, of course, and some have written long books insisting that this or that Masonic concept must be interpreted this way or that, but the Craft itself is a structure of symbols which are interpreted only in a moral sense within the rituals and teachings; what other spin each brother puts on them is up to him.

The phrase "the Great Architect of the Universe" is a good example. It's a bit of symbolism that is used by each Mason to represent the deity he worships. (You have to worship a deity to become a Mason, so the issue with atheism doesn't arise.) To a Christian or Jewish Mason, the Great Architect of the Universe is God; to a Muslim Mason, he's Allah; to a Hindu Mason, he's Brahma; to a Druid Mason, he's Hu the Mighty, and so on. It basically functions as an X in algebra, into which any appropriate number can be inserted.

comments.title: Coronavirus and astrology

replies.title: Re: Coronavirus and astrology
replies.content:
Not JMG, but my take on it:

Per JMG, the discovery of a new planet has a spin-up period of one Saturn cycle where its influence is coming into play, prior to its actual discovery. The demotion likewise has a spin-down period of one Saturn cycle *after* the demotion where its influence is waning toward whatever background noise minor planets have.

In each of these Saturn cycles, there is a Saturn opposition (which in a human life seems to be associated with rebellion, testing limits, etc.). During the spin-up period, the Saturn opposition occurred shortly after WWI broke out, and the first Saturn opposition *after* Pluto's discovery occurred late in WWII, probably as nukes were being contemplated. We happen to be approaching the Saturn opposition in this spin-down period, which seems to suggest to me that something Plutonian should be happening or about to happen.

As for Saturn-Pluto, there have been four conjunctions since Pluto's spin-up period:
- Oct. 1914 in Cancer
- July 1947 in Leo
- Nov. 1982 in Libra
- Jan. 2020 in Capricorn

Given the dates in question and what happened around that time (WWI and the breakup of the old world order in 1914, the start-up of the Cold War around 1947, the effective inauguration of what we think of as "the modern world" in 1982) what this effectively suggests to me is that "The geopolitical realities to which you have grown accustomed over the past 37 years are in the process of changing; pay attention."

- Barrigan

replies.title: Re: Coronavirus and astrology

replies.title: Re: astral and mental bodies
replies.content: Your physical body developed before your etheric body -- it was in existence in the womb before you had a separate etheric body at all. Your etheric body developed before your astral body -- it takes some time after birth, when a separate etheric body comes into being, before infants begin to develop an active emotional life. Your astral body developed before your mental sheath -- and of course neither you nor I have spiritual bodies yet. Similarly, the physical body dies first; the etheric body dies several days afterwards (the Second Death); the astral body takes longer to dissolve, and the mental sheath is the last to fade out, leaving the soul to cycle on to its next incarnation. All very straighforward!

comments.title: The SOP and internet pornography
comments.content:
Hello all

I'm a regular reader posting anonymously as I'm a little self conscious about the subject.

I'd like to add my experience of the effects of SOP practice on internet pornography addiction.

I had struggled for many years, with only intermittent success, to cut internet porn out of my life altogether. I once, with the help of those No Fap forums, managed to go well over a year just on will power, but it eventually snuck it's way back in. And this was a habit going back close to twenty years, I reckon.

All of this appears to have completely changed since I began practising the full SOP. I am quite gobsmacked. I can date the change specifically to the week I did my first performance of the SOP with Spirit Within added, back in October 2019.

It is as if a switch has been flicked, and the subtle-but quickly-unstoppable urge that used to descend on me whenever I was tired, relaxing at the computer with my blogs and Youtube (resulting in on average about 2 - 2.5 sessions of porn use a week, taking my whole porn consumption career together) is just gone. GONE.

As mentioned above, this is not yet the longest I've lasted without, but the experience of simply not being gripped by an urge to look at internet porn *at all* week in, week out is something I have not experienced EVER since the habit became a proper ingrained habit. There is no effort involved; I just don't have the urge.

As said, I'm rather gobsmacked, and rather curious.

If this is due to some external entity, as speculated in recent threads, then I'm not as perceptive as the person who began this theme here. I always assumed that the sudden snowballing urge that snuck up on me with such dreary persistence almost every time I was sat up late with my laptop was just "the nature of addiction". But now I'm not so sure.

That may not be the reason of course, but either way I am especially intrigued that this effect came into being precisely when I finished adding the final element of the ritual and not before. What is the meaning of that?

Needless to say I am happy and very grateful to you JMG for promoting this practice. I am also not being complacent; for all I know my vice could sneak up on me again. But for the moment a new reality obtains, quite simply, and even though I have had no flashing lights or dramatic effects during my SOP practice, I can affirm that TSW......

Thank you.

replies.title: Re: The SOP and internet pornography
replies.content: Thank you for the data points! It's not surprising at all that the effect cut in when you invoked Spirit Within. This is meant among other things to help you become more aware of the higher aspect of yourself -- the aspect that existed long before your body was born and will still exist long after your body dies. If your former pornography addiction was in conflict with your higher self, then making contact with your higher self could well have the result of catalyzing sudden change -- it very often does.

comments.title: the way I'm doing the SOP
comments.content:
This is a progress report, not a question. I took up the version of the Sphere of Protection that JMG posted in this blog as the installments of the instructions were posted. My intention was, for once in my life, to take up a daily practice and stick with it.

Within a month of beginning daily SOP, I had two near misses from traffic accidents that were partly my fault and would have been disastrous for me and a passenger if the cars had actually collided. That was a strong incentive to keep it up. The internal effects have been more subtle. Probably this is because I have been doing other practices that have points in common with the SOP for a long time, but irregularly. Since taking up the SOP, I feel more cheerful and balanced, also more relaxed.

Once in awhile I am eager to do the SOP, but usually I put it off and have to force myself by saying, no food until you do the SOP.

I was having trouble visualizing the expansion of the Sphere. I could visualize it extending in front of me and to the sides, but was having difficulty doing the same in the directions where my physical eyes don't see, behind me and below my feet. I added a set of gestures to help. After I visualize the expansion, I extend my arms to the sides at shoulder level, palms up, and visualize the Sphere extending about a foot beyond my fingertips. I turn my upper torso left so I'm facing sideways, extend my arms with palms down, and visualize the Sphere extending past my fingers behind and in front of me. Then I hold my right arm straight up and my left arm straight down, like the Magician trump, and visualize the Sphere extending above my head and an equal distance below my feet. This projects a sphere about twelve feet in diameter.

I fiddled with the deity sets and have settled on Apollon, Demeter and Artemis for the opening, the Titans Gaia and Helios for Spirit Below and Above, nothing for Spirit Within because I'm calling on myself. For the elements, Hu, Weland, Melusine, and Janicot. These are pre-Christian deities of western Europe, respectively Welsh, Saxon, Gallic or French, and Basque. Hu and Janicot became associated with witches in the Middle Ages and I have invoked them in ritual. Weland, at least Kipling's depiction of Him in Puck of Pook's Hill, is a blacksmith. Melusine is a water deity sometimes depicted as a two-tailed mermaid. I had not worked with Melusine previously but from what I read of Her online, She seemed approachable.

comments.title: Assorted Questions
comments.content:
First, a few questions stemming from my ongoing practice of the Dolmen Arch:

1. The grade I'm currently working through provides a pair of initiation ceremonies to be performed on others. I've taken the liberty of performing the first one on myself, and am wondering: is this likely to be effective, or is it somewhat like putting a fan on a toy sailboat? Am I simply circulating power through a closed system?

2. Two weeks ago I asked about the healing practice from the Dolmen Arch (https://ecosophia.dreamwidth.org/79397.html?thread=7304485#cmt7304485). I have two follow-up questions:

a) If the "gunk" (dense ethers) are being shaken off of the hands after a pass, what happens to that gunk? Does it just sit there in the space and get eaten by the etheric equivalent of decomposing microbes? Is it dangerous?

b) I've been contemplating the mechanism of the healing practice, and have settled on this as my working theory: Flowing the energy from the hands into the body is kind of like running clean water into a dirty sponge. The sponge fills with the clean water, and the dirt is displaced. So as I pass my hands over my or another's body, the nwyfre flowing from my hands into the body is displacing whatever gunk is in the body, and some of this gunk ends up on my hands. Is this an accurate metaphor?

3. I'm working through the last grade of the Dolmen Arch, and have been surprised by a friend of mine asking to be a student (once I've finished the course). I was caught off-guard by this, and am still not quite decided. I wanted to ask, though: What general advice would you give to a new teacher of such a course?

So far I've settled on a two-fold core of: The student needs to do (and choose to do) the work themselves (the work can only be done and chosen by the student), and also that every student's journey through this or any other course is unique (and therefore I should work to support a student's work, rather than trying to "shape" or "direct" it). Does this seem sound? What else might you add?

And then lastly an unrelated question:

4. I've been contemplating designing a ritual from first principles, and am wondering how to deal with pre-existing "tracks in space." In particular, I am considering using the unicursal hexagram in the ritual, but want to avoid as much as possible tapping into the egregore of Thelema. Do you have any advice in this regard?

Thanks!

replies.title: Re: Assorted Questions
replies.content:
1) It's a little bit like putting a fan on a toy sailboat, but as a way to learn how to set the sails, that has its value, you know.

2a) Either it gets munched or it gets dissolved. There are very simple, almost mindless entities called larvae (The word was used for spirits before it was used for baby insects.) They function as scavengers on the etheric plane, gobbling up cast-off masses of concentrated etheric substance. Sunlight and some other factors also dissolve etheric concentrations, so what the larvae don't get, the sunlight does. ;-)

2b) Yes.

3) I'm far from sure I have any business offering advice as a teacher, as it's not something I'm very good at. My approach has always been to hand over the lessons, field questions, and leave it at that. Your mileage may vary. Certainly, your proposed twofold core sounds good.

4) That's a tall order, since they've worked so extensively with it. You might be better off finding a symbol that doesn't have that baggage.

comments.title: Magical genetic engineering
comments.content:
Hi John, I'm a long time reader (since the 2014 Archdruid report days, and I have several of your books too) but this is my first comment.

I have quite a specific question for you regarding magic - namely, is it possible to change one's genes and/or physiology? This is partly an academic question, as, similar to the "just so" stories, can a given organism direct its own evolution (I'm talking in biological, not spiritual terms here) by 'wanting to' do something, either affecting its own life and/or those of its decendents? Partly it's also a more personal one - my wife has a genetic connective tissue disorder, and I wondered if dedicated focus may be able to, if not change her genes, then at least change the expression of them to correctly produce collagen. I also ask as I have heriditary male pattern alopecia (ie. My dad is bald and I'm going that way too) and would be keen to reverse it. I'm kind of guessing your answer will probably be a "yes, it is possible, but within limits", in which case, what is the best magical advice for dealing with long-term health conditions?

I haven't seen this kind of question asked on here before, but apologies if you're repeating yourself - there are a lot of comments to read through each week!

replies.title: Re: Magical genetic engineering
replies.content:
No, I'm sorry to say that won't be my answer. One of the basic maxims of magic says that the planes are discrete and not continuous; phrased less gnomically, that means that if you want to make something happen on the material plane, you have to use material plane means to do it. Magic is the art and science of causing changes in consciousness in accordance with will; when it has effects on the material plane, they're indirect, and happen because the change in consciousness influenced one or more conscious beings to do something on the material plane.

You can't do that with your genes. You got the genes for male pattern alopecia when you were conceived; they're in every cell in your body, and no magical practice will change that. Similarly, the issue with your wife's connective tissue is hardwired into her cells; you may be able to work magic to see to it that she encounters treatments that will help her or dietary changes that will ameliorate the symptoms, but Lamarckism doesn't work even when you apply magic to it. Sorry!

replies.title: reconnecting with ancestors
replies.content:
I do very little ancestor work and have no special expertise, but I have a suggestion. It might be something that is not practical for you to do, but if you can do it, I think it will work.

If your paternal grandparents were buried (rather than cremated), and it is possible for you to visit their grave, do so. Bring flowers or liquor or whatever other offering they would have expected. (If they would not expect anything, you can skip this and just put a stone on the grave before you leave.) Also bring a token that is associated with you in some way to leave at the grave.

Also, just in case, bring along something you know how to use to send away the unwelcome spirits in case they show up, (which is unlikely, but be prepared), such as holy water, a protective incense, bay leaves, Florida Water, or whatever else you use for protection or banishing at home.

Spend some time at the grave and attempt to commune with your grandparents there. If you are successful, let them know about the interruption, that you miss them and you made the trip in order to reconnect. Place the offering, whatever it is, on the grave and leave your personal token where it won't blow away or be removed. Explain that circumstances do not allow you to make regular visits to their grave but that you will invite them to visit you at home later, as you have done in the past.

If the interfering spirits follow you to the grave, stand your ground, because the grave of your grandparents is a place where you have a right to be and they do not. Mischievous spirits have no power over you when you are on your ancestral ground. Command them to leave and not come back. Reinforce the banishment with banishing pentagrams or whatever material substance you brought with you to protect the grave.

It's possible that you will not get into communication with any spirit at all. If that happens, you can still use the token you are leaving at the grave site as a magical link at Samhain.

Before you leave, say goodbye to your ancestors, look around for a pebble or small stone and place it on the grave marker. Once you have done that, walk away without looking back, and wash your hands at the first opportunity.

comments.content:
I posted awhile back about struggling with geomantic readings and not getting even remotely accurate results, as if the geomantic Oracle personally despised me. After stepping back a bit and doing reflection to sort of clear the air, I was able to resume the practice with a very much improved level of communication, including obvious signs that even I couldn't miss. So to anyone feeling similarly frustrated with your studies, I hope this inspires you to keep coming back, even if you need a breather to see things freshly.

Also, really DON'T stop your daily banishings... My discovery in that regard is that it's taking me way longer to get back to where I was than the time I took "off", so a definite net negative!

One final story. I quit practicing Christianity in my youth and for various reasons have had loads of anger toward that faith, which was always under the surface until this happened: after an awkward blow-up at an office Christmas party, I had a very vivid dream in which I had a really pleasant and enlightening chat with Jesus, much to my surprise! I rarely have interesting or even remembered dreams so this was definitely out of the norm. Wish fulfillment, Jungian Shadow thing or whatever, I'm no longer at all angry toward Christians, which is a great relief. It did feel real, even hyper-real, like our host describes encounters with these much greater Beings, though thankfully it wasn't scary (awe-full) as I would have imagined. And He didn't mention anything about me preferring Greek deities or whatever. It was like a chat with a very accepting, loving big brother. No doubt this sounds questionable but it was absolutely unexpected, unsolicited and transformative. And no, I'm still a polytheist. 😁

So thank you, JMG and the readers here, these practices have illuminated my life as they have many others.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: I'm delighted to hear this. As far as Jesus is concerned, I know a lot of polytheists who have had encounters with him, and everybody says that he's exactly what you'd expect a god of unconditional love to be like. :-)
